full_text_rationale: Full text proposes BEAM, a bi-level LLM-powered heuristic/code design system with Adaptive Memory storing reusable generated function components and a knowledge-augmentation pipeline, evaluated across combinatorial and continuous optimization benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Chuyang Xiang, Yichen Wei, Jiale Ma, Handing Wang, Junchi Yan
titulo: BEAM: Bi-level Memory-adaptive Algorithmic Evolution for LLM-Powered Heuristic Design
venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems / arXiv preprint (2604.12898)
formato_armazenamento: Adaptive Memory stores high-performing generated function components with names/descriptions so future algorithm structures can import and recombine them; Knowledge Augmentation also uses HeuBase component repositories and KnoBase text knowledge.
framework_agente: BEAM bi-level memory-adaptive algorithmic evolution framework
benchmarks_utilizados: TSP, BPP, CAF, MIS, CVRP, BBOB, PMSP and related heuristic-design benchmarks
metricas_desempenho: Optimality gap, objective value, runtime/time or token budget, average and variance of final gaps, token-consumption trajectory
baseline_comparado: EoH, ReEvo, MCTS-AHD, AlphaEvolve, LLaMEA, LLaMEA-HPO, KaMIS, RLSA, ARW, HGS, Split and local search, EACO-EDM, BE without Adaptive Memory, one-shot and MCTS education baselines
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: NR; token budgets and token-consumption trajectories are reported for some experiments, including 55w token CVRP budget.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Adaptive Memory is updated during evolutionary resets with high-quality generated functions; HeuBase is intentionally compact and designed to grow through community contribution.
custo_por_tarefa: NR as monetary cost; experiments report time budgets from 18 minutes to 5 hours and token-budget experiments up to 55w tokens.
reducao_tokens: Qualitative: Adaptive Memory lets the LLM call stored functions instead of regenerating long code blocks, shrinking emitted code; no exact token-reduction percentage extracted.
resultado_principal: BEAM reduces the optimality gap by 37.84% in aggregate on CVRP hybrid algorithm design and generates competitive or superior heuristics across MIS, CVRP, TSP, BBOB, and other optimization benchmarks.
desempenho_vs_baseline: In CVRP, BEAM with Split and local search reports gaps of 0.09%, 0.38%, and 0.86% for CVRP-100/200/500 versus EoH gaps of 0.22%, 0.57%, and 1.09%. In BBOB, BEAM average gap is 0.007 versus ReEvo 0.957, EoH 6.032, and LLaMEA-HPO 0.386.
custo_vs_baseline: BEAM needs more tokens before generating its first heuristic in complex tasks due to the bi-level structure, but Adaptive Memory is intended to reduce repeated code emission; no monetary baseline is reported.
limitacoes: Bi-level structure can require larger initial token consumption; framework may add unnecessary complexity for single-function generation; some comparisons use repository best heuristics with unknown budgets.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark heterogeneity, budget matching, unreproduced baseline results, LLM/model differences, token/time budget settings, and code-execution failures in initial generations may affect validity.
